message:
feat(mux): add is_cancelled() introspection to DirectMuxError

Add a public method to detect whether a DirectMuxError represents an
explicit Cx capability-context cancellation vs other I/O errors. The
detection checks for ErrorKind::Interrupted with "cancelled" in the
message, matching the pattern produced by checkpoint_mux_cx().

This enables callers (pool retry logic, circuit breakers) to distinguish
"the caller cancelled this operation" from "the network failed" without
pattern-matching into the error internals.

Two tests:
- cancelled_mux_io_is_detected_without_changing_protocol_bucket: Verifies
  is_cancelled() returns true for the canonical cancellation error AND
  that protocol_error_kind() still classifies it as Transient (no
  change to existing retry classification).
- generic_interrupted_io_is_not_treated_as_mux_cancellation: Verifies
  that a plain Interrupted error without "cancelled" in the message
  returns false, preventing false positives from signal-based interrupts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
